elements.sent-2411-055: Video Autoencoder Variational autoencoder (VAE)~\cite{vae projects input images or videos into a compressed latent space obeying a certain distribution and maps the latent sampled from the obtained distribution into RGB space with a decoder.}

elements.sent-2411-058: We denote the temporal down-sampling factor of a VAE as $f_t$ and the spatial one as $f_s$.
elements.sent-2411-059: Given an input video $\mathcal{V}$ with the shape of $3 \times T \times H \times W$, the compressed latent $z_{\mathcal{V }$ as the input into diffusion models fall into the space of $|z| \times T/f_t \times H /f_s \times W/f_s$, where $z$ denotes the latent channel.}
elements.sent-2411-060: Thanks to the superior performance of open-source Stable Diffusion (SD)~\cite{stablediffusion models, it has been a common practice to leverage SD-VAE to process images and videos into compressed latent.}

elements.sent-2411-062: As adjacent video frames share large similarities in pixel space, video signals are inherently more redundant than images.
elements.sent-2411-063: Based on this idea, we adopt a more aggressive down-sampling factor for video autoencoder.
